releaseGate.Notes: Full score refresh after all major cycles
Split from generic UI shell before final gate

priorityRows.metric: Audio identity, event feedback, and cue alignment
priorityRows.why: Largest current score gap and high user-experience impact: shots, explosions, boss damage, challenge results, capture/rescue feedback.
priorityRows.next: Run a longer audio segmentation/model-assisted analysis cycle.

priorityRows.why: Controls whether long play feels like Galaga-like escalation rather than repeated pressure.
priorityRows.next: Apply one narrow Stage 12 reward candidate and rerun the frozen conformance loop.

priorityRows.metric: Overall visual look and feel: gameplay, start page, typography complexity
priorityRows.status: Estimated; needs dedicated visual conformance scorer
priorityRows.why: A high score can still feel off if start text, density, contrast, alien readability, and arcade typography do not cohere.
priorityRows.next: Create a visual-look scorer covering start/attract page, gameplay readability, typography density, color discipline, and reference contact sheets.
priorityRows.evidence: UI shell suite plus generated frame/contact-sheet artifacts

priorityRows.metric: Stage 4 pressure exact replay / pressure curve precision
priorityRows.why: Pressure should be learnable and reproducible, not merely present in one run.
priorityRows.effort: Medium-high; prior runs ~12.8 min wall / 18.5 min CPU
priorityRows.next: Run focused source-window replay matching after the Stage 12 loop validates candidate mechanics.

priorityRows.metric: Alien entry to levels: formation, timing, and methods
priorityRows.status: Composite proxy: stage opening timing + geometry + movement grammar
priorityRows.why: Entry formations and rack timing are a first-order arcade authenticity signal before combat even starts.
priorityRows.next: Promote alien-entry as its own scored submetric; compare stage-entry contact sheets and timing traces across early/mid/late levels.

priorityRows.metric: Challenge-stage variation and new alien/formations introduction
priorityRows.status: Composite proxy: challenge timing + challenge identity + non-repetition
priorityRows.why: Challenge stages should teach new motion/reward patterns, not only pause normal combat.
priorityRows.next: Add a challenge-variation metric for alien type introduction, path families, result feedback, and bonus opportunity clarity.

priorityRows.why: Keeps the game learnable across skill levels and supports later-stage quality.
priorityRows.next: Resolve remaining ordering edge case after higher-value audio/level-arc work.

priorityRows.why: First impression and direct reference feel.
priorityRows.next: Tune only after audio and level-arc priorities unless regressions appear.

priorityRows.status: Measured as UI shell; needs separate arcade-frame style rubric
priorityRows.why: The cabinet frame is the constant product surface around every game.
priorityRows.next: Split frame style from generic shell integrity: rails, bezel density, labels, chroming, build/date treatment.

priorityRows.status: Measured through UI shell suite; needs modal-specific scoring
priorityRows.why: Popup surfaces carry learning, scoring trust, feedback, and player records.
priorityRows.next: Add modal-specific scorer for help, scoring, feedback, account, leaderboard, and game-over result screens.

priorityRows.why: Protects user trust while pressure is increased.
priorityRows.effort: Guardrail; 30-90 min per risky gameplay cycle
priorityRows.next: Keep as required guardrail for pressure/reward changes.

priorityRows.why: Core control feel is already excellent.
priorityRows.next: Do not tune unless a new reference metric proves a gap.

priorityRows.why: Core combat response is already excellent.
Strong Galaga identity mechanic; should not regress while feedback improves.
priorityRows.next: Use as release blocker for capture/rescue-adjacent audio or explosion changes.

priorityRows.why: Timing is strong; variation is the gap, not baseline timing.
priorityRows.next: Preserve while adding challenge variation scoring.

newFirstClassAxes: Alien entry to levels: formation layout, timing, path method, and whether different stages enter differently.
Challenge-stage variation: new alien types, new entry formations/styles, path families, reward/result feedback, and teaching value.
Overall visual look and feel: gameplay readability, start/attract typography density, copy complexity, color discipline, and reference contact sheets.
Arcade console frame UI: cabinet frame, bezel/rails, build/date trust signals, button density, and arcade-style containment.
Popup/help/scoring surfaces: help, scoring, leaderboard, account, feedback, and game-over result formatting as their own modal-quality family.
maintenanceRules: Refresh this artifact after each full quality score, investment-priority run, or major conformance loop.
Keep the local dashboard generated from this artifact data; do not link or publish it through player-facing Platinum surfaces until explicitly approved.
Treat rows marked estimated/composite as measurement debt: useful for planning, but not release-proof until backed by a harness.
Keep user-facing release gates separate from harness-learning wins. A rejected candidate still belongs in artifacts when it teaches the loop what not to keep.
Prefer work with a large score gap, high user-experience impact, reusable ingestion/harness value, and clear guardrails.
